content.title: Export Payment Terms — Risk and Recommendations

terms.name: L/C (Letter of Credit)
terms.description: Bank-guaranteed payment. Seller receives payment upon presentation of compliant documents. Best protection for new or unknown buyers.
terms.bestFor: New buyers, large orders, high-risk countries
terms.drawbacks: Higher bank fees, complex documentation, slow processing

terms.name: T/T (Telegraphic Transfer)
terms.description: Wire transfer. 30-50% advance payment with balance on B/L or against documents. Common for established relationships.
terms.drawbacks: Partial risk if buyer defaults on balance payment

terms.name: Doc. Collection (Documentary Collection)
terms.description: Bank handles document exchange. D/P (Documents against Payment) or D/A (Documents against Acceptance). Less secure than L/C.
terms.bestFor: Buyers in countries with strong banking systems
terms.drawbacks: Buyer may refuse documents; bank doesn't guarantee payment

terms.name: Open Account
terms.description: Goods shipped before payment. Payment due in 30-90 days. Highest risk for exporter.
terms.bestFor: Long-established, trusted buyers in low-risk countries only
terms.drawbacks: Full payment risk; requires export credit insurance

recommendations.newBuyer: Always use L/C or minimum 50% T/T advance
recommendations.highRiskCountry: L/C only — do not accept open account
recommendations.exportInsurance: Consider LPEI (Indonesia Eximbank) export credit insurance for open account
